title: Best Collectr Alternative for MTG Collection Tracking
description: Best Collectr alternative for MTG collection tracking. Scan cards, organize inventory, track prices, and manage your Magic collection with more context in Spellvault.

hero.title: Collectr Alternative for MTG
hero.description: Choose Spellvault if you want a Collectr alternative with card scanning, stronger inventory structure, and price tracking connected to the collection.

sections.title: The best Collectr alternative for MTG
sections.description: Spellvault gives MTG collectors more than a portfolio snapshot by combining scanning, organization, and price tracking in one workflow.

items.title: Track value with more context
items.description: See price movement and collection value without separating those numbers from the cards, copies, and structure behind them.

items.title: Scan new cards into your collection
items.description: Bring new cards into Spellvault faster, so collection updates do not turn into slow manual entry work.

items.title: Organize real inventory structure
items.description: Manage folders, decks, binders, and storage in a way that gives you more control than a simple portfolio view.

sections.title: Spellvault vs Collectr
sections.description: A quick look at the differences that matter if you need more than a simple value snapshot.

sections.title: Frequently Asked Questions
sections.description: Helpful answers for people comparing Spellvault and Collectr.

items.title: Is Spellvault a good Collectr alternative?
items.description: Yes. Spellvault is a strong Collectr alternative if you want price tracking plus better scanning, clearer inventory structure, and more control over your collection.

items.title: What makes Spellvault different from Collectr?
items.description: The main difference is that Spellvault goes beyond a portfolio view and gives you a more connected workflow for scanning, organizing, and managing cards.

items.title: Can I scan cards in Spellvault?
items.description: Yes. Spellvault includes card scanning, which makes it easier to add new cards without turning every update into manual entry.

items.title: Can I track price and inventory together in Spellvault?
items.description: Yes. Spellvault keeps pricing connected to the collection itself, so price data is easier to use when you are managing real inventory.

items.title: Who should switch from Collectr to Spellvault?
items.description: Collectors, sellers, and traders who want stronger collection structure, faster intake, and more practical inventory control should consider switching.

items.title: Is Spellvault better for large MTG collections?
items.description: It can be a better fit when your collection grows beyond a simple portfolio and you need search, storage structure, duplicates, and organization to stay clear.

items.title: Can I organize binders, decks, and boxes in Spellvault?
items.description: Yes. Spellvault is built to support real collection structure, which is useful when cards live across decks, binders, folders, and storage boxes.

items.title: Why choose Spellvault if I need more than a portfolio app?
items.description: Spellvault is better suited for people who want scanning, collection organization, pricing, and inventory actions in one connected MTG workflow.
